full_name: Key FISH Functions for PFC 5.0 Scripting
description: Essential FISH intrinsic functions for PFC 5.0 scripting. Covers wall, ball, contact, and measurement functions with signatures and usage examples.

sections.heading: Wall Functions
sections.content:
Access wall properties via wall pointer (wp).

  wall.find(id)
    Returns: wall pointer
    Args: integer wall ID
    Usage: wp = wall.find(3)

  wall.pos(wp, dir)
    Returns: position component (float)
    Args: wp = wall pointer, dir = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local zpos = wall.pos(wp, 3)

  wall.disp(wp, dir)
    Returns: cumulative displacement component (float)
    Args: wp = wall pointer, dir = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local dx = wall.disp(wp, 1)

  wall.force.contact(wp, dir)
    Returns: total contact force component on wall (float)
    Args: wp = wall pointer, dir = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local fx = wall.force.contact(wp, 1)

  wall.id(wp)
    Returns: wall ID (integer)
    Usage: local wid = wall.id(wp)

  wall.near(px, py, pz)
    Returns: wall pointer nearest to position (px, py, pz)
    Usage: wp = wall.near(0, 0, 0)

  Wall history to FISH mapping (PFC 5.0):
    history wall xcontactforce id N  ->  wall.force.contact(wall.find(N), 1)
    history wall ycontactforce id N  ->  wall.force.contact(wall.find(N), 2)
    history wall zcontactforce id N  ->  wall.force.contact(wall.find(N), 3)
    history wall xdisplacement id N  ->  wall.disp(wall.find(N), 1)
    history wall ydisplacement id N  ->  wall.disp(wall.find(N), 2)
    history wall zdisplacement id N  ->  wall.disp(wall.find(N), 3)

sections.heading: Ball Functions
sections.content:
Access ball properties via ball pointer (bp).

  ball.list()
    Returns: array/list of all ball pointers
    Usage: loop foreach bp ball.list ... end_loop

  ball.near(px, py, pz)
    Returns: ball pointer nearest to position (px, py, pz)
    Usage: bp = ball.near(0, 0, 0.04)

  ball.pos(bp, dir)
    Returns: ball position component (float)
    Args: bp = ball pointer, dir = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local x = ball.pos(bp, 1)

  ball.pos.x(bp)
    Returns: ball x-position (float) — shorthand
    Usage: local x = ball.pos.x(bp)

  ball.pos.y(bp)
    Returns: ball y-position (float) — shorthand

  ball.pos.z(bp)
    Returns: ball z-position (float) — shorthand

  ball.radius(bp)
    Returns: ball radius (float)
    Usage: local r = ball.radius(bp)

  ball.disp(bp, dir)
    Returns: cumulative ball displacement component (float)
    Args: dir = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local dx = ball.disp(bp, 1)

  ball.xv(bp)
    Returns: ball x-velocity (float)

  ball.yv(bp)
    Returns: ball y-velocity (float)

  ball.zv(bp)
    Returns: ball z-velocity (float)

  ball.force.contact(bp)
    Returns: total contact force magnitude on ball (float)

  ball.id(bp)
    Returns: ball ID (integer)

  ball.group(bp, slot_name)
    Returns: group name string for given slot
    Usage: local g = ball.group(bp, 'material')

sections.heading: Measurement Functions
sections.content:
Access measurement sphere properties via measurement pointer (mp).

  measure.find(id)
    Returns: measurement sphere pointer
    Args: integer measurement ID
    Usage: mp = measure.find(1)

  measure.create(id, rad, x, y, z)
    Returns: measurement sphere pointer
    Note: Use 'measure create' command instead for clarity.

  meas.stress(mp, i, j)
    Returns: stress tensor component (float)
    Args: mp = measurement pointer, i, j = 1(x), 2(y), 3(z)
    Usage: local sxx = meas.stress(mp, 1, 1)
    
    Common components:
      meas.stress(mp, 1, 1)  — sigma_xx
      meas.stress(mp, 2, 2)  — sigma_yy
      meas.stress(mp, 3, 3)  — sigma_zz
      meas.stress(mp, 1, 2)  — tau_xy
      meas.stress(mp, 2, 3)  — tau_yz
      meas.stress(mp, 1, 3)  — tau_xz

  measure.porosity(mp)
    Returns: porosity (float, 0.0-1.0)
    Usage: local poro = measure.porosity(mp)

  measure.coordination(mp)
    Returns: average coordination number (float)
    Usage: local cn = measure.coordination(mp)

sections.heading: Contact Functions
sections.content:
Access contact properties via contact pointer (cp).

  contact.find(id)
    Returns: contact pointer
    Usage: cp = contact.find(3)

  contact.pos(cp, dir)
    Returns: contact position component
    Usage: local x = contact.pos(cp, 1)

  contact.force.n(cp)
    Returns: normal contact force magnitude (float, positive = compression)
    Usage: local fn = contact.force.n(cp)

  contact.force.s(cp)
    Returns: shear contact force magnitude (float)
    Usage: local fs = contact.force.s(cp)

  contact.gap(cp)
    Returns: gap distance (float, negative = overlap)
    Usage: local g = contact.gap(cp)

  contact.surf1(cp)
    Returns: first surface pointer (ball or wall)
    Usage: local s1 = contact.surf1(cp)

  contact.surf2(cp)
    Returns: second surface pointer (ball or wall)
    Usage: local s2 = contact.surf2(cp)

  contact.type(cp)
    Returns: contact type string ('ball-ball' or 'ball-wall')
    Usage: local ctype = contact.type(cp)

sections.heading: Global and Utility Functions
sections.content:
global.dim
    Returns: model dimension (integer, 2 or 3)
    Usage: if global.dim = 2 then ... end_if

  mech.age
    Returns: mechanical age (cumulative time or step count)
    Usage: local age = mech.age

  mech.step
    Returns: current step number (integer)
    Usage: local step = mech.step

  mech.timestep
    Returns: current critical timestep (float)
    Usage: local dt = mech.timestep

  domain.min.x()
    Returns: minimum x-boundary of domain (float)
    Usage: local xmin = domain.min.x()

  domain.max.x()
    Returns: maximum x-boundary of domain (float)
    
  domain.min.y(), domain.max.y()
    Returns: y-boundaries of domain

  domain.min.z(), domain.max.z()
    Returns: z-boundaries of domain

sections.heading: Math Utility Functions
sections.content:
math.pi
    Returns: constant pi (3.14159...)
    Usage: local circumference = 2.0 * math.pi * r

  math.abs(f)
    Returns: absolute value of f
    Usage: local absvel = math.abs(vel)

  math.max(a, b)
    Returns: maximum of a and b
    Usage: local xmax = math.max(xmax, x)

  math.min(a, b)
    Returns: minimum of a and b
    Usage: local xmin = math.min(xmin, x)

  math.sqrt(f)
    Returns: square root of f
    Usage: local dist = math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy)

  math.exp(f)
    Returns: e^f

  math.log(f)
    Returns: natural logarithm of f

  math.sin(f), math.cos(f), math.tan(f)
    Returns: trigonometric functions (f in radians)

  math.asin(f), math.acos(f), math.atan(f)
    Returns: inverse trig functions (result in radians)

  math.degrad
    Returns: degrees-to-radians conversion factor (pi/180)
    Usage: local rad = 45.0 * math.degrad

sections.heading: Random Seed and Simulation Control
sections.content:
set random N
    Sets the random seed for all subsequent stochastic operations
    Usage: command ... set random 10001 ... end_command
    Note: Must be used inside a command block

  set gravity gx gy gz
    Sets gravitational acceleration vector
    Usage: command ... set gravity 0 0 -9.81 ... end_command

  set hist_rep N
    Sets history recording interval (every N steps)
    Usage: command ... set hist_rep 10 ... end_command

sections.heading: IO and Output Functions
sections.content:
io.out(string)
    Prints a string to the console
    Usage: io.out('Current stress: ' + string(sigma))

  io.write(filename, string)
    Writes a string to a file (overwrites)
    Usage: io.write('results.txt', 'stress = ' + string(sigma))

  io.read(filename)
    Reads file contents as string — advanced usage

  string(val)
    Converts a number to string
    Usage: local s = string(3.1415)

  file.open(name, mode, handle)
    Opens file with mode 'r' (read), 'w' (write), 'a' (append)
    Usage: file.open('output.dat', 'w', 1)

  file.write(handle, string)
    Writes string to open file handle
    Usage: file.write(1, 'data line\n')

  file.close(handle)
    Closes file handle
    Usage: file.close(1)

sections.heading: While_Stepping Pattern
sections.content:
The 'while_stepping' keyword inside a FISH function registers it as a callback that executes at the end of every mechanical timestep.

Pattern:
  define my_function
    while_stepping
    ... body executes every step ...
  end

Multiple while_stepping functions execute in the order they were defined.

Common uses:
  - Servo control (wall velocity adjustment each step)
  - Dynamic area update (recompute cross-section each step)
  - Custom history tracking (check values each step)
  - Threshold detection (halt condition monitoring)

Important notes:
  - while_stepping functions are automatically called by the solver; do not call them manually
  - They execute AFTER the mechanical update and AFTER the default history recording
  - To disable, define a version without while_stepping or use a flag:
      define my_servo(flag)
        if flag = 1 then while_stepping end_if
        ...
      end
  - Performance: keep while_stepping functions efficient. Avoid loops over all balls unless necessary. Use 'set fish_safe 0' only if you're certain about thread safety.

sections.heading: Practical Notes
sections.content:
- Direction indices: 1 = x, 2 = y, 3 = z in ALL FISH functions, regardless of 2D or 3D mode.
- Wall pointers vs IDs: Use wall.find(id) once and store the pointer for efficiency. Calling wall.find(id) every step is slower than storing the pointer.
- ball.list() returns a dynamic list that updates as balls are created/deleted. Cache it in a variable if you need a snapshot.
- meas.stress() returns the average stress tensor over all particles intersecting the measurement sphere. The sign convention follows the engineering sign (positive = tension in some versions; check your specific PFC 5.0 build).
- All FISH functions use single-precision floats unless explicitly using double. Be aware of precision limitations for very small numbers.
- The 'while_stepping' pattern is the PFC 5.0 way to implement per-cycle logic. In PFC 7.0+, this has been replaced with event-driven 'fish callback' registration.
- FISH variables are global by default unless declared as 'local'. Use 'local' for loop counters and temporary variables to avoid namespace pollution.
